sections.heading: About this Task
sections.body:
- You can use any syntax that works with the `update` parameter of
  [db.collection.updateMany()](manual/reference/method/db.collection.updateMany).

- The **Update Documents** modal does not support any `options`
  parameters such as upsert, writeConcern, or collation.

- Previews of the documents affected by bulk update operations are
  only visible if your database is configured to support transactions.
  For details, see [/core/transactions](manual/core/transactions).

sections.heading: Step 1: Apply a query filter
sections.body:
- Select the collection.

- From the **Documents** tab, input a query into the
  **Query bar**. The filter criteria of the query
  specified applies to the documents in the
  **Bulk Update** modal. If you need to apply an update
  to all documents in a collection, leave the **Query bar** blank.

sections.heading: Step 2: Open the bulk update modal
sections.body:
On the **Documents** tab, click the Edit
**Update** button to display the
**Update Documents** modal. The following
table summarizes the UI (User Interface) of the modal:

| UI Element | Description                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ---------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Filter     | Any filter criteria specified on the Query Bar applies to the Update Documents modal. To update the filter query, exit the Update Documents modal and modify the query in the Query Bar. |
| Update     | The update syntax that is applied to the documents specified in the filter criteria. You can use any syntax that works with the update parameter of the db.collection.updateMany().      |
| Preview    | A preview of documents with the update syntax applied.                                                                                                                                   |

sections.heading: Step 3: Enter the update syntax
sections.body:
In the **Update** text field, provide the update
syntax.

The number of documents affected by the update displays at
the top of the **Update Documents** modal.

> **Note:** The documents under the **Preview** header show how the
> **Update** syntax affects documents in your collection.

sections.heading: Step 4: Update your documents
sections.body:
Click **Update Documents**.

Atlas applies the **Update** to the documents
within the **Filter** expression.

sections.heading: Example
sections.body:
The following example uses the
[Load Sample Data Into Atlas](sample-data).

This example updates the `tomatoes.viewer.numReviews` and
`tomatoes.viewer.meter` fields with the Atlas
**Update Documents** modal.

Apply a filter in the **Query Bar** to filter movies which
have a `year` of `1917`.

```javascript
{ 'year' : 1917 }
```

Click the Edit **Update** button, the **Update Documents**
modal displays.

In the **Update** text box, paste the following syntax:

```javascript
{
   $inc: { "tomatoes.viewer.numReviews" : 1},
   $set: { "tomatoes.viewer.meter" : 99 }
}
```

This syntax:

- [Increments](manual/reference/operator/update/inc) the
  `tomatoes.viewer.numReviews` field by `1`.

- [Sets](manual/reference/operator/update/set) the
  `tomatoes.viewer.meter` field to `99`.

The **Preview** section populates with sample documents
affected by the update query.

To view the updates to the **numReviews**
and **meter** fields:

- Click the angle-right right arrow icon next to
  **tomatoes**.

- Click the angle-right right arrow icon next to
  **viewer**.

Click the **Update Documents** button to update the collection's
data.
